review_comment:
_One worthy note for reviewer (as the diff is a bit hard to read unfortunately)_: Previously, this function _always_ asserted that there is the `super !== null` check before `super` is invoked. This assertion won't be generated in the downleveled ES2015 output as the constructor seems explicitly called to TS when it processes the ES2015 output to ES5.

I've updated the logic to skip the `super !== null` check if there is no binary expression, but if there is, then we still enforce that this is actually the expected `super !== null` check. I'm not sure of any risk here as we even ensure that there are no parameters on the containing constructor (for which we perform this check).
One thing that would be very helpful both for reviewers and future readers of this code would be to include examples of the shape of code being matched by these functions/conditionals.

I had such examples for all of these functions; but there are quite a lot of these and it felt even worse repeating the JSDoc examples multiple times (resulting in inconsistency and difficulity to maintain). I'll check if I can add a smaller one here.
@alxhub added back more concrete examples to all functions (with concrete descriptions). It's more to maintain; but should be okay given that the synthetic delegate output isn't changing too often.
Sure! I use these conventions too, but this is just moved code that already existed. Seems like something to cleanup separately for the compiler team. WDYT?
These _could_ be standalone functions outside of the class. I'm okay with either.
Yeah, both could work, but I moved the code explicitly into the class so that I can rely on the host for detecting the spread helper, without having to pass through the host X times.
Feel free to combine multiple guards into a single `if` block.
This is code that just moved around, and I didn't want to make a lot of unrelated changes/cleanups.
